hot_topics.synthesis: Slate Auto has officially launched its online configurator for a new electric truck, confirming a sub-$25,000 starting price. This vehicle, which promises an EPA range of 205 miles, is generating buzz as an affordable entry into the electric pickup market. Supported by Jeff Bezos, this debut aims to offer a radically simple and accessible EV option.

OpenAI has unveiled its first custom-designed AI processor, named Jalapeño, developed in collaboration with Broadcom. This Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) is specifically engineered to power current and future large language models, focusing on enhancing AI inference capabilities. The development signifies OpenAI's strategic investment in its own hardware infrastructure to optimize AI performance.

Zoox, Amazon's autonomous vehicle company, has revealed a refreshed design for its purpose-built robotaxi as it prepares for mass production and commercial service. The updated vehicle features enhanced interior elements like cushioning, lighter colors, and improved audio systems for communication with support. Zoox is continuing its efforts to secure federal approval to operate these autonomous vehicles without traditional driver controls.

Western Europe is grappling with a severe, record-breaking heatwave, particularly impacting France, Spain, and Italy, leading to power outages in France and discussions about increasing air conditioning use. Across the continent, people are adopting various strategies, from setting up cool-down spots to applying chalk on windows, to cope with the soaring temperatures. Switzerland is also experiencing tropical-like heat, affecting daily life and public events.

hot_topics.topic: Iran Nuclear Inspections and US War Powers
hot_topics.synthesis: The head of the UN's nuclear agency confirmed that inspectors will visit Iranian nuclear enrichment sites as part of an interim deal between Iran and the U.S., despite conflicting statements from both nations regarding the arrangement. Concurrently, the U.S. Congress passed a war powers measure related to Iran, marking a symbolic break from the former Trump administration's foreign policy approach, though its practical impact is limited.

hot_topics.topic: Democratic Socialists' Momentum in NYC Primaries
hot_topics.synthesis: Candidates backed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani achieved a significant sweep in recent Democratic primaries, reinforcing the growing influence of the anti-establishment progressive left within the Democratic Party. The Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) are leveraging this momentum, with leaders actively planning to back a presidential primary candidate for 2028, signaling an intent to expand their national political footprint. These victories have also highlighted ongoing political divisions in the city, particularly concerning issues like the Israel-Gaza war and public safety policies.

items.title: The memories of others
items.summary: A video explores how a Japanese war photographer became an insightful chronicler of "the Troubles" in Northern Ireland.

items.title: A curious crossover: The Toyota C-HR review
items.summary: Toyota's new C-HR electric crossover is reviewed, featuring a twin-motor AWD setup with 338 hp and a 74.7 kWh battery pack, starting at $37,000. It is smaller than the bZ4x but not significantly cheaper with its standard high-performance powertrain.

items.title: Odd police video shows drone removing knife from motionless suspect
items.summary: The Sacramento County Sheriff’s Office released a video showcasing a drone using a magnet to disarm a motionless suspect holding a knife, an action it described as a "nationwide first." This incident involved a felony suspect who was non-responsive to negotiators.

items.title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments
items.summary: Oracle reduced its workforce by 21,000 employees in the past year, attributing these layoffs in part to the adoption and deployment of AI technologies across its operations. The company's annual filing noted a 12.9 percent reduction in full-time staff.

items.title: Slate Auto's truck builder goes live for its $25k electric pickup
items.summary: Slate Auto has launched an online configurator for its electric truck, confirming a starting price of $24,950. The vehicle features a 63 kWh lithium iron phosphate battery, 181 hp electric motor, and an EPA range of 205 miles.

items.title: White House drastically shortens deadline for dropping quantum-vulnerable crypto
items.summary: The White House has accelerated the deadline for government agencies to transition to quantum-resistant encryption systems, now requiring adoption for high-value assets by December 31, 2030, and quantum-safe digital signatures by December 31, 2031. This move comes as new research indicates lower-than-expected costs for building cryptographically relevant quantum computers.

items.title: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge
items.summary: Savannah Guthrie publicly appealed for help on NBC's Today show after details emerged about a note regarding her missing mother, Nancy Guthrie, claiming she had died.

items.title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
items.summary: France is re-evaluating its historical reluctance towards air conditioning as temperatures soar to record levels, leading to a political discussion on its wider adoption.

items.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
items.summary: Sevastopol, Crimea's largest city, experienced a widespread power outage following Ukrainian strikes. The Moscow-installed governor warned that some areas might remain without electricity until the evening.

items.title: Blood ties and opportunity: Why more World Cup players than ever are not representing their birth nations
items.summary: Nearly a quarter of the footballers participating in the 2026 World Cup are not representing their birth nations, a trend driven by blood ties and opportunities.

items.title: Bondi Beach shooting hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on his father
items.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed, hailed as a hero for tackling a gunman in the Bondi Beach shooting, has pleaded not guilty to an alleged assault on his father.

items.title: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba sues US government over defence blacklist
items.summary: Alibaba, a Chinese e-commerce giant, is suing the US defense department after being added to a blacklist of firms with alleged ties to the Chinese military.

items.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
items.summary: Candidates supported by New York's Zohran Mamdani achieved a clean sweep in the Democratic primary, with Brad Lander unseating Dan Goldman. This outcome highlighted divisions in the city over the Israel-Gaza war.

items.title: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat
items.summary: Senator Iván Cepeda, a left-wing presidential candidate in Colombia, conceded defeat after being narrowly beaten by right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella by less than a percentage point.

items.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
items.summary: Congress passed a war powers measure for the first time, signaling a break with former President Trump's stance on Iran, although experts suggest it holds little power and is mainly politically significant.

items.title: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot
items.summary: Eight individuals were sentenced to a combined 450 years in prison for their involvement in an anti-ICE riot in Texas, during which an officer was shot in the neck by "Antifa Cell operatives."

items.title: France confirms first Ebola case
items.summary: France has confirmed its first Ebola case, involving a French doctor who had been working in DR Congo, where over 260 deaths have been reported from the disease.

items.title: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
items.summary: A French woman was rescued in Pakistan after allegedly being held captive for 12 years by her husband, along with her five children. She claimed they were isolated from the outside world.

items.title: From cool-down spots to chalk on windows - how Europe is coping with the heat
items.summary: Across Europe, people are adopting various strategies, from seeking cool-down spots to applying chalk on windows, to cope with the severe heatwave gripping the continent.

items.title: From startups to global spotlight - Indian entrepreneur takes charge of WhatsApp
items.summary: Indian entrepreneur Kunal Shah, a prominent figure in India's startup ecosystem, is now leading WhatsApp, placing him in a global spotlight.

items.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
items.summary: Germany's national rail network, Deutsche Bahn, experienced a nationwide halt for over two and a half hours due due to an IT malfunction.

items.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
items.summary: Kenyan authorities plan to charge 16 students, aged 15 to 18, with murder in connection with a dormitory fire at Utumishi Girls' School last month that killed 16 pupils.

items.title: Power outages hit France as record heatwave set to peak
items.summary: A record heatwave in France has caused power outages, affecting approximately 68,000 homes in Brittany. Full electricity restoration is not expected until Wednesday night.

items.title: Stanford graduates rethink their futures as AI transforms tech
items.summary: Stanford University graduates are re-evaluating their career paths as the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence reshapes the technology sector.

items.title: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack
items.summary: Leah Stewart, a 34-year-old Sydney woman, woke from an induced coma over a week after a shark attack at Coogee Beach, which resulted in the amputation of one of her arms.

items.title: Temperatures hit record levels in western Europe
items.summary: Western Europe, particularly France, Spain, and Italy, is experiencing record-breaking temperatures as a severe heatwave intensifies.

items.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
items.summary: The UN nuclear chief, Rafael Grossi, stated that IAEA inspectors would visit Iranian sites as part of a war deal, though an Iranian minister linked such access to a final agreement with the US.

items.title: Why are there holiday delay warnings over the EU's new border system?
items.summary: Warnings of holiday delays are emerging due to the EU's new, delayed Entry/Exit System, which will alter travel procedures for UK passengers entering 29 countries.

items.title: "Fix" MacBook Neo Cursor Lag: Record 1 Pixel of the Screen Every 10 Seconds
items.summary: A proposed "fix" for MacBook Neo cursor lag involves recording one pixel of the screen every 10 seconds. This unconventional method suggests an underlying issue that requires unusual workarounds.

items.title: Founding a company in Germany: €9600, 152 days and I still can't send an invoice
items.summary: An article details the arduous process of founding a company in Germany, reporting a cost of €9600 and 152 days with the founder still unable to send an invoice. The article implies significant bureaucratic hurdles.

items.title: Qwen-AgentWorld: Language World Models for General Agents
items.summary: The paper "Qwen-AgentWorld" explores the concept of language world models for creating general AI agents. This research focuses on developing agents capable of understanding and interacting with various environments.

items.title: Raspberry Pi Pico W as USB Wi-Fi Adapter
items.summary: A project demonstrates how to use a Raspberry Pi Pico W microcontroller as a USB Wi-Fi adapter. This provides a compact and flexible solution for adding wireless connectivity via USB.

items.title: Vulnerability reports are not special anymore
items.summary: An article argues that vulnerability reports have lost their special status, suggesting a shift in how they are perceived or handled.

items.title: We're making Bunny DNS free: because a faster internet won't build itself
items.summary: Bunny.net announced it is making its Bunny DNS service free, stating the decision is driven by a commitment to a faster internet.

items.title: All challenges big and small
items.summary: This editor's letter reflects on human ingenuity in overcoming challenges, drawing parallels between the massive reconstruction efforts in Kuwait after the 1991 Gulf War and current scientific and engineering endeavors. It highlights the ambition required to tackle problems ranging from nanoscale chip manufacturing to planetary-scale climate engineering.

items.title: China is winning the other tech race
items.summary: This analysis argues that while the U.S. leads in AI, China is dominating in "electric technology." Drawing on historical patterns of great powers mastering key technologies, it suggests China's strides in electric tech represent a significant competitive advantage.

items.title: 5 years after the Surfside condo collapse, the toll of the tragedy remains
items.summary: Five years after the Surfside condominium collapse killed 98 people, Florida marks the enduring impact of one of the largest structural failures in U.S. history.

items.title: Congress passes major housing bill. And, Mamdani-backed candidates sweep NYC primaries
items.summary: Congress has passed a significant housing affordability bill, while in New York City, all three candidates endorsed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani won their respective primaries.

items.title: Greetings from sweltering Switzerland
items.summary: Switzerland is experiencing tropical-like temperatures, with soccer fans in Lucerne watching a World Cup match under sweltering conditions.

items.title: IAEA chief says inspectors will visit nuclear sites under Iran-U.S. interim deal
items.summary: The head of the UN's nuclear agency confirmed that inspectors would visit Iranian nuclear enrichment sites as part of an interim deal between Iran and the U.S. This statement followed conflicting reports from both nations.

items.title: Political consultant on whether Trump is losing GOP support after war powers vote
items.summary: A Republican political consultant discussed whether former President Trump is losing support among congressional Republicans following the Senate's vote to limit his war powers concerning Iran.

items.title: Star Fox Review: Can't quite teach an old Fox new tricks
items.summary: A review of the Star Fox remake for Switch 2 praises its high-effort visuals and fun battle mode but criticizes its campaign for feeling outdated.

items.title: Understanding 'masculinism,' a movement to restore the primacy of men
items.summary: Masculinism is defined as a belief system asserting that feminism emasculates men and advocates for men's primacy in society, with women focused on raising children. This movement is reportedly gaining mainstream traction.

items.title: Why are crisis pregnancy centers saying they can 'rule out' ectopic pregnancy?
items.summary: A watchdog group reports that some anti-abortion Christian crisis pregnancy centers are misleading patients by claiming they can "rule out" ectopic pregnancies. These centers have received increased federal support under the Trump administration.

items.title: Will Texas' new top voting official be a 'disruptor'? Locals are preparing for it
items.summary: Texas is set to appoint a new top voting official, with the frontrunner being a state lawmaker and pastor who lacks election experience. Locals are concerned about potential disruption ahead of closely contested midterms.

items.title: Democratic socialists are coming for 2028
items.summary: Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) leaders are planning to actively back a presidential primary candidate for 2028, following sweeping victories in New York City. They are conducting surveys among their 250 chapters and will hold national discussions to decide on an endorsement.

items.title: How Physicists Track and Trap the Elusive Neutrino
items.summary: This article recounts the historical "Project Poltergeist" from 70 years ago, an experiment by physicists Clyde Cowan and Frederick Reines to detect neutrinos using a custom-built 10-ton detector near a nuclear reactor.

items.title: datasette 1.0a35
items.summary: Datasette 1.0a35 introduces significant updates, including new "Create table" and "Alter table" interfaces and JSON APIs for database management. It also features improved documentation for template context variables, treating them as a stable API.

items.title: OPFS + Pyodide test harness
items.summary: A new test harness explores the possibility of Datasette Lite editing persistent SQLite files directly in the browser using OPFS (Origin Private File System) and Pyodide. This aims to enable in-browser data persistence for the Python application.

items.title: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures raises victorious $3B fund
items.summary: Menlo Ventures successfully raised a $3 billion fund, solidifying its reputation as an AI investor after a significant $750 million investment in Anthropic in 2024.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
items.summary: Anthropic's new Claude Tag feature integrates an always-on AI teammate into Slack, aiming to boost productivity and capture organizational context. This tool is seen as a strategic move to accumulate institutional knowledge and streamline enterprise workflows.

items.title: How to invest when everything is moving too fast
items.summary: A TechCrunch event in Los Angeles featured two prominent AI investors discussing investment strategies in a rapidly evolving technological landscape. The discussion provided both entertainment and insight into current AI investment trends.

items.title: If you want to cut your screen time, just get a Brick
items.summary: The "Brick" is presented as a simple, $59 plastic device designed to help users reduce their screen time. The article suggests it offers a solution for those struggling to curb digital dependency.

items.title: India’s MoEngage bets that the future of marketing is millions of AI agents
items.summary: MoEngage, an Indian company, believes the future of marketing lies in utilizing millions of AI agents, acquiring technology that assigns individual AI agents to customers through an all-cash deal.

items.title: Klue says hackers stole credential from 2022 that led to customer data breaches
items.summary: Klue reported that a credential stolen in 2022 from a limited pilot was later used by hackers to breach a system containing keys for accessing customer data. The company has not clarified why the credential was not revoked.

items.title: Mark Zuckerberg wants Meta to launch its own prediction market
items.summary: Mark Zuckerberg reportedly aims for Meta to launch an independent prediction market application. This app would exist separately from Meta's other social media platforms, though those sites could direct users to it.

items.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced its first custom processor, named Jalapeño, developed in collaboration with Broadcom and specifically tailored for the inference systems of OpenAI's AI models. This ASIC is designed to meet the unique computational demands of advanced AI.

items.title: Slate Auto’s radically simple electric truck starts at $24,950
items.summary: Slate Auto, backed by Jeff Bezos, has revealed pricing details for its debut electric vehicle, with the pickup variant starting at $24,950 and an SUV variant at $29,950. The base model offers a range of 205 miles.

items.title: Superhuman acquires AI detection startup GPTZero
items.summary: Superhuman has acquired GPTZero, an AI detection startup, further enhancing its capabilities in this area. Superhuman already offers an AI detection tool as part of Grammarly.

items.title: This new tracking label could help solve cargo theft
items.summary: Samsara has introduced a new tracking label that discreetly incorporates a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) system for real-time location tracking in disposable packaging. This innovation aims to combat cargo theft.

items.title: Valor Equity Partners looks to raise a $2.5B Fund VII, per Bloomberg
items.summary: Valor Equity Partners is reportedly seeking to raise $2.5 billion for its seventh fund, Fund VII, following an earlier announcement last year about raising unspecified capital.

items.title: Walmart-backed Flipkart expands quick-commerce push as Amazon ramps up in India
items.summary: Walmart-backed Flipkart has expanded its quick-commerce operations by exceeding 1,000 micro-fulfillment centers, intensifying its competition with Amazon in the Indian market.

items.title: Zoox upgrades its robotaxi as it prepares for commercial service
items.summary: The new Zoox robotaxi has been upgraded with improved cushioning, lighter interior colors, and enhanced microphone and speaker systems for better communication with support. These changes precede its preparation for commercial service.

items.title: Great Americans: Matthew Henson at the North Pole
items.summary: This article details the controversy surrounding the first person to reach the North Pole in 1909, contrasting Robert Peary's claim with Matthew Henson's account of leading the sledge and arriving first. Henson, Peary's African American assistant, received less recognition during his lifetime.

items.title: Mamdani’s Socialist Sweep in New York
items.summary: Leftist candidates backed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani won all their Democratic primaries, strengthening the party's anti-Zionist and defund-the-police wing. These wins, featuring candidates like Darializa Avila Chevalier and Claire Valdez, are seen as part of a "hostile takeover" of the Democratic Party.

items.title: Mr. President, Here’s How to Fix Your Pool
items.summary: The Reflecting Pool in Washington D.C. faced issues of turning green and peeling lining shortly after a rapid renovation announced by President Trump. A pool consultant discusses potential causes and solutions for these problems.

items.title: This Week in American History: New York’s Underdogs Prepare to Fight
items.summary: This historical account details the arrival of the massive British fleet in New York City in June 1776, after the evacuation of Boston, as General George Washington and his Continental Army prepared for an anticipated offensive.

items.title: Why Is the Media Ignoring the Montreal Shooter’s Antisemitism?
items.summary: This article questions why media coverage is overlooking the antisemitic aspects of recent attacks in Canada, including one in Montreal's Jewish community that killed a police officer and a Jewish civilian. It notes heightened tensions amid a "crisis of antisemitism" in Canada.

items.title: Diatoms and the Meaning of Life
items.summary: This article explores the delicate beauty and vital ecological role of diatoms, unicellular algae that produce nearly half of Earth's oxygen and form the base of the planet's biomass. It highlights the work of naturalist Adolf Schmidt's "Diatom Atlas," which chronicled these microscopic wonders.

items.title: 8BitDo’s all-button controller gets a screen upgrade for customizations without an app
items.summary: 8BitDo has released a new Arcade Controller Pro, which is thinner and includes a small screen for on-device button mapping and settings customization. This leverless design replaces joysticks with buttons for improved precision.

items.title: OpenAI reveals its first AI processor: Jalapeño
items.summary: OpenAI has partnered with Broadcom to create its first "intelligence processor" chip, named Jalapeño, designed as an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) for AI inference. This chip aims to power current and future large language models.

items.title: Zoox’s purpose-built robotaxi is getting a refresh
items.summary: Zoox, an Amazon-owned autonomous vehicle company, has revealed a refreshed design for its bidirectional robotaxi, intended for mass production. The company is seeking federal approval to operate these vehicles without traditional controls as it expands its free robotaxi service.
